message:
chore(beads): close franken_numpy-kuh8

All mechanically-fixable categories drained:
- B: 3 tests (pyo3 0.28 call_method0 → getattr) — 83e668a
- C: 2 tests (FP-strict → numpy.allclose) — 83e668a
- D: 1 test (numpy.lib.recfunctions submodule) — 83e668a
- E: 1 test (bytes(str) encoding) — 0c20c56
- F: 1 test (assert_approx_equal tolerance) — 0c20c56
- H: 2+2 tests (bool display + unit-tests) — 83e668a

Umbrella integration core_conformance_suites_pass is an aggregator
over pre-existing fnp-conformance fixture-id-drift (tracked outside
this bead).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"franken_numpy-ksr","title":"[MEGA] Close all remaining parameter-level parity gaps","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-23T07:24:39.060586047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:30:49.409870372Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:30:49.409469593Z","close_reason":"Closed all implementable parameter-level parity gaps:
- GROUP 1: unique_axis() for multi-dimensional uniqueness along axis (2 tests)
- GROUP 2: concatenate_with_dtype + stack_with_dtype with casting validation (2 tests)  
- GROUP 3: reduce_sum_initial + reduce_prod_initial with initial value (2 tests)
- GROUP 5: percentile_keepdims + quantile_keepdims (3 tests)
Plus earlier: clip_optional, reshape_order, histogram_full (7 tests)
Total: 16 new tests, 8 new methods. Remaining gaps are architectural (out param, dtype accumulation, structured arrays).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-ktac","title":"Wire FFT metamorphic conformance suite","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:02:03.055689008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T07:14:37.793805396Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T07:14:37.791986755Z","close_reason":"Wired FFT metamorphic conformance suite","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"franken_numpy-kuh8","title":"[HIGH] 11 workspace test failures: pyo3 0.28 test-side bugs + numpy 2.x API drift + rust-port FP strictness + bool format drift","description":"Ran `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-fail-fast` on HEAD (27a595d + 21a46a9 + 59430e9 stack) with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc. Found 11 non-preexisting failures across 3 crates + 6 failures already tracked in UPGRADE_LOG.md (hermite/laguerre/ma_count + 3 fnp-conformance fixture-id-drift failures — unchanged, not part of this bead).

## Category B — pyo3 0.28 upgrade test-side bugs (3 tests)

Tests call `.call_method0("__name__")` on type objects. In pyo3 0.28, `call_method0` invokes the attribute AS A METHOD. `__name__` is a string attribute on Python type objects, not a method, so it raises TypeError on invoke. Fix: replace with `.getattr("__name__")` (10 sites total in lib.rs test module).

- tests::common_type_matches_numpy_across_single_mixed_int_float_complex_and_error — "'str' object is not callable"
- tests::linalg_eig_matches_numpy_across_real_complex_diagonal_and_batched — "'numpy.ndarray' object is not callable"
- tests::roots_poly_matches_numpy_across_real_complex_matrix_trim_and_roundtrip — "'numpy.ndarray' object is not callable"

## Category C — Rust-port FP precision drift (str() comparison too strict) (2 tests)

cod-numpy's rust-port of window functions (hanning/hamming/blackman in commit 75b13a8, kaiser in d208811) produces ULP-level differences from numpy's C implementation. Python's repr() of floats emits different trailing digits based on binary-exact value. Test compares via `str(list) == str(list)`. Values are correct within FP tolerance but str repr differs.

- tests::hanning_hamming_blackman_match_numpy_across_small_odd_even_and_edge_m — diff at digits 16-17, e.g. 0.1882550990706332 vs 0.18825509907063326
- tests::kaiser_matches_numpy_across_m_edges_beta_range_and_shape_dtype — diff at digit 11, e.g. 0.03671089217588313 vs 0.036710892271286676

Fix: replace str-based equality with numpy.allclose(ours, theirs) assertion. Either relax tests (preferred — floats should never be compared via str) OR audit the rust-port math to eliminate the ULP drift.

## Category D — numpy 2.x API removal (1 test)

- tests::recfunctions_family_matches_numpy_across_drop_rename_append_merge_and_u2s — AttributeError: module 'numpy.lib' has no attribute 'recfunctions'

numpy 2.x moved recfunctions to top-level numpy.lib.recfunctions namespace differently OR removed it. Fnp-python wrappers still call numpy.lib.recfunctions.<name>. Fix: use numpy.lib.recfunctions import path that works on numpy 2.x (possibly import numpy.lib.recfunctions as separate submodule) or swap to numpy.rec.

## Category E — numpy 2.x API signature drift (1 test)

- tests::fromstring_matches_numpy_across_sep_whitespace_dtype_count_and_errors — TypeError: string argument without an encoding

numpy.fromstring(bytes-like) was deprecated in numpy 1.14, likely still functional but the test passes a str without encoding which the binary-mode fromstring now requires in numpy 2.x. Fix: pass encoding or use bytes/bytearray at the call sites in the test (the wrapper itself may be fine).

## Category F — testing_assert precision (1 test)

- tests::testing_assert_family_matches_numpy_across_pass_fail_and_surface — AssertionError: Items are not equal to 7 significant digits: ACTUAL: 123456.7, DESIRED: 123456.8

Sounds like an intentional 'must-fail' branch of the test that is not being caught. Fix: wrap the expected failure in a catches pattern, OR adjust the expected-failure tolerance.

## Category H — Bool format drift in fnp-ufunc (2 tests)

Rust's Display for bool emits lowercase 'true'/'false'; numpy oracle emits Python 'True'/'False'. Mismatch at oracle formatting step.

- tests::masked_minimum_fill_value_matches_live_numpy_oracle_when_available — left: 'bool:true' vs right: 'bool:True'
- tests::masked_maximum_fill_value_matches_live_numpy_oracle_when_available — left: 'bool:false' vs right: 'bool:False'

Fix: format bools as 'True'/'False' when the dtype branch is Bool in the oracle formatter — crates/fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs:47409 and :47518 (or the shared helper that feeds both).

## Reproduction

```
export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-fail-fast
```

Expected: 11 fnp-python + 2 fnp-ufunc new failures documented above, plus 3 fnp-conformance fixture-id-drift failures + 3 pre-existing convention failures (hermite/laguerre/ma_count) already tracked as separate issues.

## Priority rationale

P1/HIGH — these are test failures on HEAD; CI would be red. Categories B/H are mechanical fixes (~15 min total). Categories C/D/E need small wrapper/test tweaks (~30 min each). Category F needs test audit. No user-visible functional regression in the wrappers themselves.","notes":"Partial drain by cc-numpy: Categories B (3 pyo3 call_method0 tests), C (2 FP-strict tests), D (recfunctions submodule), H (bool display drift) all fixed in commit 83e668a — 10/10 target tests now ok. REMAINING: Category E (numpy.fromstring encoding drift — 1 test) and Category F (testing_assert_approx_equal expected-failure branch — 1 test). Umbrella integration test core_conformance_suites_pass is an aggregator over 3 pre-existing fnp-conformance fixture-id-drift failures (linalg_differential_cases invalid fixture id linalg_cholesky_solve_identity_L_returns_b) — those are tracked in UPGRADE_LOG.md 2026-04-22 session notes as fnp-conformance's responsibility.  {"id":"franken_numpy-kxkp","title":"numpy.testing.assert_allclose python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.testing_assert_allclose passthrough wrapper around numpy.testing.assert_allclose(actual, desired, rtol=1e-7, atol=0, equal_nan=True, err_msg='', verbose=True). Raises AssertionError if not all-close. Include oracle test covering: identical arrays passes silently, within-tol passes, beyond-tol raises AssertionError, equal_nan True/False parity, custom err_msg appears in raised message, and broadcasting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:24:18.841974180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:29:02.998495866Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:29:02.996208147Z","close_reason":"Completed in b63b0ba: numpy.testing.assert_allclose wrapper with 7-scenario oracle test covering pass/fail paths, NaN/equal_nan, custom err_msg, and broadcasting","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
